books.title: The Little Friend / The Secret History
books.author: Donna Tart

books.title: The Hounding
books.author: Xenobe Purvis
books.notes: NYT January book club pick. Beautiful descriptive writing. You feel the cracked summer dryness. This is easily translated into the thirst of the villagers for violence. But I think this is ultimately a story about women and power. It's a story about women told primarily through the eyes of men. About women who have no real control over their lives and at the same time too much freedom. I love the open ended ending and the way it shows that whatever was true never mattered. It is only what people believe that matters.

books.notes: Loved. I enjoy time fracturing stories immensely generally and nice to find one with a fresh take.i didn't see the twist but really should have.

books.notes: I struggled to get through it, did not really understand the hype but some compelling parts to the story.

books.title: Tomorrow, and Tomorrow, and Tomorrow
books.author: Gabrielle Zevin
books.notes: Loved.

books.title: My Year of Rest and Relaxation
books.author: Ottessa Moshfegh
books.notes: Oh how I loved this. So uncomfortable. Such an unlikable main character but one that has resonated with me. I relate to this desire to sleep through recovery from a trauma. And I really just enjoyed having a main character who isn’t perfect or special or trying to become a better person. She is just on a selfish journey and at the end she is still just as selfish.

books.title: A Visit from the Goon Squad
books.author: Jennifer Egan

books.title: Mother Tongue
books.author: Bill Bryson
books.notes: Interesting. But boring to read so much on the topic.

books.title: All Fours
books.author: Miranda July
books.notes: For the excellently executed humour and another flawed and complicated woman, loved it.

books.title: I Who Have Never Known Men (FR)
books.author: Jacqueline Harpman
books.notes: The story is so chilling, endless analogies can be made but for me inner story that is most resonant is the question of what it really means to be human when everything else is stripped away, what can we learn, what do we not need to be taught and what cant we when we have no reference to society as it is today, and how much perspective plays a role in how we choose what to value and what has value. If you've never lived in today's world, you can value the other one more than those who have experienced it. And a final flawed (but this time strong) female main character to end off 2025.

books.title: What We Can Know
books.author: Ian McEwan
books.notes: Struggled to rad it, perhaps not used to the way Ian McEwan writes. At times I felt he was being repetitive and droning on about the same thing. But I loved the story and the more I thought about it afterwards, the more I have come to appreciate it. The premise is excellent.

books.title: This Is Where the Serpent Lives
books.author: Daniyal Mueenuddin

books.title: Half His Age
books.author: Jennette McCurdy
books.notes: So grating and nauseating and uncomfortable. Loved the pace. Love her narration. Devoured it in two days.

books.title: People We Meet on Vacation
books.author: Emily Henry
books.notes: I don't enjoy romcoms in book form as much as I love them in movie form

books.title: The Unlikely Pilgrimage of Harold Fry
books.author: Rachel Joyce

books.title: Wuthering Heights
books.author: Emily Brontë
books.notes:
Surprisingly did not want to put this down. I remembered struggling through pages in earlier life. But it is disturbing and I had some bad nights sleep with very animated nightmares while reading this. Most probably unrelated, but the darkness and wildness sits deeply within one while reading it. I am relieved it is over and happily. That I did not expect.
I don’t think of this as a love story. It never made me feel anything sweet or even desirous - if that is a word. It cannot compare as a lover story to Pride and Prejudice or any other Austen. We are not witness to the apparent supposed development of love between C+H when they are kids, we only assume it must be through the third hand retelling of Nelly, during their playing together on the moors, and then it is suddenly declared by them each as adults to each other. It never felt personal or real or like love. More like possession, or some kind of childish and immature comfort brought about by shared trauma. Love, I never saw love in this story except for the love of Hindley for his daughter and perhaps Nelly over all her charges at some point. And possibly younger Cathy and Hareton at the end, although even that love seems to exist out of necessity and boredom and a lack of other options than as a love that I understand. Not a love story, but a great story, nonetheless.

books.author: Rufi Thorpe
books.notes: Nothing special in the writing, but I really enjoyed the storyline. An easy, flowy, beachy read.

books.title: The Great Gatsby

books.notes: Absolutely loved it. Made me rethink rereading more classics.

books.title: Good Material
books.author: Dolly Alderton
books.notes: Nice easy read, not laugh out loud funny except for the one scene with the therapist but I enjoyed it anyways. The end, where the narrator switches to Jen’s perspective and retells the story from her pov reminded me a lot of Fleishman is in Trouble, which I loved.

books.title: Normal People
books.author: Sally Rooney
books.notes: After such a struggle getting through Intermezzo, I can't believe how I flowed through this. Could not stop reading.

books.title: Yesteryear

books.notes: Loved.

books.title: On the Calculation of Volume III
books.author: Solvej Balle

books.title: Dungeon Crawler Carl
books.author: Matt Dinniman
books.notes: Great narrator. Fun story.

books.title: Strangers
books.author: Belle Burden
books.notes: She is not the best narrator, or writer. But I was interested in her story.

books.title: Writers and Lovers
books.author: Lily King
books.notes: I floated above this story for most of it, probably because of my own mental state than because of the story. But only in the final 20% did I feel like I was finally there with it and found myself crying through the final pages. It was a flowing read and did not require much of me, which was nice. The feeling was there, but lightly, and I would have liked more. But I will read the next one.
